privileged_info_summary: The task uses a customer journey map for e-commerce returns based on synthetic but well-researched content. The specification file references real e-commerce research organizations (Baymard Institute and Narvar), and key statistics are verifiable: 92% of customers with easy returns will repurchase (Narvar); 67% of shoppers check return policies before buying; Baymard research confirms 54% of sites have substantial returns UX issues. The diagram uses correct HTML color codes: #d5e8d4 (positive), #fff2cc (neutral), #f8cecc (negative). Five journey stages are properly defined: Discover Return Policy, Initiate Return, Prepare & Ship, Await Processing, Receive Resolution. All 52 key phrases across the five stages are present in the specification. The data is entirely synthetic (generated in setup scripts) but grounded in real e-commerce research methodologies.
